mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) transmit rising labor income into higher consumer spending and corporate revenues, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133999.0 as of t-1mo indicates persistent hiring growth
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS changes and 20-day SPX forward returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and debt service costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.5% as of t-1mo exceeds the 3.0% threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal collapsing consumer and residential investment demand, amplifying downside earnings risk and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 350106.0 and HOUST at 2042.0 as of latest prints
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining RSAFS and HOUST does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of triggered instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, a declining UNRATE signals accelerating job market recovery that boosts consumer confidence and cyclical earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of t-1mo is below 6.0% so the signal is currently inactive
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 6.0% and falling, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) reflects rising manufacturing utilization and operating leverage, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 119.678 as of t-1mo indicates robust factory output
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO growth does not exhibit a positive Rank IC with 5-day SPX forward returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in retail sales (RSAFS) below its trailing 3-month average signals weakening consumer demand that leads to negative revenue surprises and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS below its 3-month average coincides with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with stagnant real GDP (GDPC1) create a stagflationary environment where margin compression and valuation derating produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 4.0% and GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 196.1 and GDPC1 at 11088.6 as of latest prints
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL growth and low GDPC1 growth fails to produce negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) alongside a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals an aggressive central bank response to an overheating labor market, tightening financial conditions sharply and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.5% and UNRATE at 4.9% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: The combination of rising FEDFUNDS and falling UNRATE does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of triggered months.
